name: Nasa
description_short: NASA Federal Credit Union is a $5B+ asset credit union headquartered in Maryland, serving 250,000 members nationwide with competitive rates on auto loans, mortgages, and certificates.
description_long:
NASA Federal Credit Union was founded on September 29, 1949, originally as the NACA Washington Federal Credit Union by employees of the National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics. When NACA became NASA in 1958, the credit union followed suit, eventually settling on its current name in 1974. The organization is insured by the National Credit Union Administration and holds over $5 billion in assets. In 2019, NASA Federal merged with The Partnership Federal Credit Union, expanding its branch network and membership base to its current 250,000 members across the United States.

NASA Federal offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, credit cards with fixed-rate balance transfer offers (9.9% for accounts opened within 90 days), auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, certificates of deposit, online banking, and investment services. The credit union operates 13 physical locations but provides access to 5,600 shared branches and 42,000 fee-free ATMs nationwide. Members also benefit from fee waivers at 53,000 partner credit unions throughout the U.S.

The credit union distinguishes itself through competitive rates recognized by major financial publications. It was named Best-in-State credit union in Maryland by Forbes in 2018 and 2019. NASA Federal is particularly noted for competitive auto loan rates (averaging two percentage points better than comparable bank figures according to The Wall Street Journal), high-yield certificates recognized by Investopedia and Business Insider, and zero-down mortgages up to $1,149,825 with no private mortgage insurance. In 2020, the credit union originated 1,166 mortgages totaling $337 million. The organization also maintains community involvement through partnerships like the annual Credit Union Cherry Blossom 10-mile run for Children's Miracle Network and a scholarship program established in 1983.

NASA Federal's primary limitation is membership eligibility—applicants must be NASA employees, relatives, retirees, or members of one of 900 approved partner companies and associations. This restricted membership model differs from traditional banks and many credit unions with open membership. While the credit union offers competitive rates and extensive ATM access, prospective members should first verify their eligibility through NASA employment or one of the listed affiliated organizations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Credit cards with balance transfer options
Loan products for NASA employees and qualified affiliates
pros: Zero-down mortgages up to $1,149,825 with no private mortgage insurance (originated $337M in mortgages in 2020)
Fixed-rate 9.9% balance transfer offers for credit cards opened within 90 days
Access to 42,000 fee-free ATMs and 5,600 shared branches nationally
Auto loan rates average 2 percentage points better than comparable bank rates per Wall Street Journal
High-yield certificates recognized by Investopedia, DepositAccounts.com, and Business Insider
Named Best-in-State credit union in Maryland by Forbes (2018, 2019)
Fee waivers at 53,000 partner credit unions nationwide
cons: Membership restricted to NASA employees, relatives, retirees, or members of 900 approved organizations—not open to general public
Limited physical branch footprint with only 13 locations (primarily Maryland/Virginia area based on merger details)
Credit card balance transfer rates (9.9%) only available to accounts opened within 90-day window
Smaller asset base ($5B) compared to major banks limits product customization and service options
best_for: NASA employees, contractors, and their families seeking competitive mortgage and auto loan rates
Members of qualifying professional associations (American Astronautical Society, National Space Society, etc.) looking for rate-competitive financial services
Borrowers interested in zero-down mortgages without PMI in the $500K-$1.1M range
Credit union members nationwide seeking fee-free ATM access through partner networks
diagnosis: NASA Federal Credit Union is best for NASA-affiliated employees and members of 900 eligible partner organizations seeking competitive rates on mortgages, auto loans, and certificates. The primary caveat is membership eligibility—this is not an option for unaffiliated consumers, making it inaccessible to the general public without qualifying employment or organizational membership.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
